query: documentation update for phase 4.1 onboarding and auth flow

results.title: Phase 4.1 requirements refined for setup and direct auth
results.content: Reviewed and rewrote docs/requirements/p4_1_dashboard_setup_and_direct_auth.md to align with Minder's actual auth model. Key correction: admin auth remains API-key based, so the requirements now target first-run /setup, admin API-key recovery via CLI, and direct client_api_key parity across transports instead of inventing password auth. The doc now distinguishes current baseline capabilities already present in code (/dashboard/login browser flow, SSE X-Minder-Client-Key, token exchange, onboarding routes) from the remaining gaps to implement. It also preserves token exchange as a supported compatibility path and records open questions around stdio bootstrap shape, session invalidation on recovery, and whether first-admin setup needs an extra deployment secret.

results.title: Phase 4.0 MCP gateway auth/dashboard design drafted
results.content: Created docs/design/mcp-gateway-auth-dashboard.md to redesign Minder onboarding around a client-aware MCP gateway. The design separates AdminUser and Client identities, adds Client/ClientApiKey/ClientSession/AuditLog entities, proposes token exchange plus simple client-key bootstrap modes, uses Redis for short-lived session state and MongoDB for durable metadata, and defines dashboard backend/frontend scope for client/API key management and onboarding templates. Also updated docs/TASK_BREAKDOWN.md with Phase 4.0 tasks and docs/PROJECT_PROGRESS.md to mark Phase 4 IN PROGRESS at design stage.

results.title: Local onboarding docs and port 8800 defaults
results.content: Rewrote the root README as a real documentation entrypoint, added docs/guides/local-setup.md and docs/guides/admin-client-onboarding.md, and switched local dev defaults from port 8080 to 8800 in config, minder.toml, Dockerfile, docker-compose.dev.yml, and generated onboarding templates in src/minder/server.py. The guides are explicit that /dashboard exists but still needs bearer auth because a browser-native admin login form is not implemented yet. Verified with mypy and targeted pytest covering config, delivery assets, phase 4 admin HTTP, and the phase 4 gateway e2e flow.

results.title: OMS API architecture docs scaffold
results.content: Created documentation set under docs/: README index, ARCHITECTURE.md, API_REFERENCE.md, DATABASE_AND_MIGRATIONS.md and linked all from root README. Docs describe clean architecture layers, module structure, runtime wiring, endpoint inventory, and migration conventions for users/roles/permissions/organizations.

results.title: Lifecycle commands synced to all agent layers
results.content: Updated all 3 layers to include Group B lifecycle commands (new-project, map-codebase, discuss-phase, plan-phase, execute-phase, ship, progress, next, milestone) and Group C utilities (todo, stats, health, note, do). Files updated: .github/copilot-instructions.md (replaced AI Workflow Commands section with 3 groups A/B/C), .agents/workflows/full-lifecycle-delivery.md (added Lifecycle Path section with per-phase loop), .agents/workflows/execute-plan.md (added lifecycle path mode detection, coder debug on error, coder note for blockers), .agents/workflows/new-requirement.md (added scope detection: small feature vs new project with coder new-project, next steps branching), .agents/workflows/update-planning.md (added coder progress/next/health/milestone audit/note integration). All workflows now aware of both quick-path (PLAN.md) and lifecycle-path (STATE.md) modes.
